outcome.confidence_rationale: Static review is thorough and all integration points (imports, helper signatures, lock discipline, reserve-before-Popen ordering, idempotency ordering, allowlists) check out; tests comprehensively map to P1-P5 plus rollback and redaction-at-rest. Confidence held below 0.95 only because I could not independently run the suite in this session (bash approval declined), so green status is self-reported—though the handoff policy explicitly sets required_evidence_grade to self_reported.

assumptions_to_verify: row['terminal_outcome_json'] key-access guard works for pre-migration rows during poll (guarded via 'in row.keys()')
redact() is idempotent so re-canonicalizing stored ledger JSON equals canonicalized cache for matching content
contradictions_checked: OSError handler calls complete_* which requires an existing row -> resolved: reserve_* inserts row before Popen
job_id threading could change S2 idempotency hash -> resolved: job_id added after idempotency_token computed
complete_* inside _write_lock calling event insert could deadlock -> resolved: _insert_event_unlocked is lock-free
discrepancy compare could be noisy on key order/secrets -> resolved: canonical_terminal_outcome_json redacts+sorts both sides, matching PRD comparison basis
missing_evidence: Actual pytest pass/fail output for the two targeted suites
Full-suite regression run confirming P5 (no gate/reviewer regressions)
critical_review.strongest_objection: The accept rests on self-reported test results: I could not execute uv run pytest (approval declined 3x), so I have not directly observed the 9 tests pass; a subtle runtime failure (e.g., _event_payload double-stamping or row_factory key access on old rows) would only surface at runtime.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Any failing test in the two targeted suites or the full regression run, or evidence that an old job row lacking the new columns raises during poll, would flip this to revise.

decisions: accept: implementation matches intent scope (migration, atomic terminal write, ledger-first poll, result.json legacy cache+backfill, ledger-wins discrepancy event) with non-goals respected
Run the two targeted test files to upgrade evidence from self_reported to independently-verified before merge
objections: Independent test execution was not performed because uv run pytest approval was declined in this session; test_status relies on self-reported grade
terminal_outcome_recorded_at is written but never read (audit-only field, non-blocking)
outcome.summary: S3a makes detached workflow terminal outcomes durable in the SQLite ledger. Static review confirms migration v3, atomic complete_dual_agent_workflow_job, ledger-first poll with legacy result.json backfill, ledger-wins discrepancy auditing, and worker-side persistence threaded after idempotency. All wiring (imports, helpers, allowlists, reserve-before-Popen) verified; non-goals respected. Accept; independent test execution blocked by env approval, self-reported grade permitted by handoff policy.
